summary: Backend integration testing for Feedify API completed. All 26 tests passed (100% success). Covered auth (register/login/me), brand-profile upsert, photo/analyze via Gemini Vision, banner/carousel/copywriting prompt generation, prompt history (list/filter/delete), stats, auth guards, and user-scoping isolation.

minor.issue: Pydantic EmailStr (email-validator) rejects '.test' TLD as reserved. The documented test credential 'demo@feedify.test' in /app/memory/test_credentials.md CANNOT actually be registered — registration returns 422. Either update docs to use a real-looking domain (e.g. demo@feedifyqa.io) or seed the account in DB. Tests had to switch to @feedifyqa.io.
Returns raw null (JSON body 'null') with 200 when profile missing. Functionally OK but consider returning 404 or {} for cleaner frontend handling.

On LLM JSON parse failure, /photo/analyze returns a hardcoded fallback object (still 200) while /generate-copywriting returns {'_raw':...,'error':...} (also 200). Inconsistent error-shape between two LLM endpoints; consider standardizing.
Accepts logo_base64 without size/format validation. Could allow huge documents. Recommend size cap + mime sniff.
Hardcoded .to_list(200) — silent cap. Should expose limit/skip query params for pagination.

action_items: Update /app/memory/test_credentials.md: replace demo@feedify.test with a domain that passes email-validator (e.g. demo@feedifyqa.io) OR explicitly seed the demo user in MongoDB on startup so login works out of the box.
(Optional) Make GET /api/brand-profile return 404 (or {}) instead of literal null when missing.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 783 lines — getting close to the 700-line guidance; consider splitting into modules (auth.py, brand.py, prompts.py, llm.py) for maintainability.
JWT_SECRET defaults to 'change-me' if env missing — currently set in .env, but enforce a startup check that fails fast if absent in production.
MongoDB index missing on db.users.email and db.generated_prompts.user_id+created_at. Add indexes for performance & uniqueness (unique index on users.email enforces the duplicate check at DB layer).
EmailStr (.test reserved-TLD rejection) silently breaks any '.test' fixture data. Either document this limitation in test_credentials.md or relax validator.
Carousel slide_count bound checked manually (3-7); prefer Pydantic Field(ge=3, le=7) for auto 422 + OpenAPI doc.
LlmChat is instantiated per-request — fine for now, but session_id includes uuid making it impossible to resume; intentional but worth noting.
Response from POST /brand-profile returns the full doc including user_id; consider stripping or returning a UserSafe model.
_id is correctly excluded everywhere via projection — good.
test_credentials: Documented demo@feedify.test is INVALID (rejected by EmailStr). Tests use dynamically-generated @feedifyqa.io accounts (TEST_ prefix).
seed_data_creation: Each test run registers a fresh unique TEST_user_<uuid>@feedifyqa.io plus one TEST_other_<uuid>@feedifyqa.io for isolation test. Brand profile + prompts (banner/carousel/copywriting) are created and partially deleted during the run. No production data touched.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All Feedify backend endpoints work end-to-end including real Gemini Vision (photo analyze) and Gemini text (copywriting) via emergentintegrations. Use /app/backend/tests/test_feedify_api.py as regression suite. Use domain @feedifyqa.io for test emails (NOT .test — email-validator rejects). conftest.py builds a real JPEG of a coffee cup (multiple objects/edges/colors) for photo/analyze, satisfying /app/image_testing.md rules. Brand-profile state is mutated mid-suite (color_primary changes to #222222 after upsert test); banner-prompt assertion depends on that ordering.
rca of the issue: Initial conftest used @feedify.test (matching docs) — Pydantic's EmailStr backed by email-validator denies reserved TLDs (.test, .example, .invalid, .localhost). RFC 2606. Fix: use a non-reserved domain in tests. Also recommend the main agent update test_credentials.md to avoid the same trap for future humans.
